title: Diversification preserves, concentration builds
html:
TL;DR

  • Three-line investing note Oct-18-2021: "Diversification preserves wealth. Concentration builds wealth. Figure out regret minimization framework model."
  • Captures the core Buffett Bezos paradox — preservation requires diversification; creation requires concentration — and notes them as a tension to hold, not resolve.
  • First explicit Bezos regret minimization framework reference in the vault — Yahir at 18 already aware of the heuristic ("imagine yourself at 80, which choice do you regret less?") and tagging it as something to figure out, not just quote.
  • Anchors the alongside Tesla vs Edison, Tesla aspiration, three things manifest, AR/VR thesis — Yahir is in a sustained framework-collection sprint.
  • Maps to Yahir's later Austin thesis move (concentrate on one bet — AI search) vs his earlier four stock COVID watchlist (diversify across pandemic plays) — the same person navigating both poles 18 months apart.

Note

Three lines. Yahir at 18 holding the diversification-vs-concentration tension and tagging Bezos's heuristic as homework. The note is a thinking-prompt, not a thesis: figure out the model is the verb. What's notable is the absence of resolution — Yahir is comfortable letting the contradiction sit, which is itself the right move (the contradiction is the lesson). The regret minimization callout becomes a recurring reference frame in Yahir's later memento mori thinking — the through-line is "decide from the future, not the present."

Connections

  • regret minimization framework — first explicit mention
  • buffett vs bezos — the dichotomy
  • memento mori decision filter — adjacent decision frame
  • the austin thesis — concentration applied
  • 2020 03 22 stock watchlist march — diversification applied

related.label: Buffett vs Bezos — preserve vs build
